question: Does an explicit per-context causal matrix beat a correlation null under P≪G?
verdict: FAIL — Â beats chance (0.624>0.5) but LOSES to a correlation null (0.828); oracle true-col=1.000. Mechanism recovery not established under P≪G.

question: Does the response 3rd moment predict what covariance cannot, on real data?
verdict: NEGATIVE — 3rd moment orthogonal to the residual; 0/24 strata significant (median ΔR²≈2.8e-06). Positive on the simulator, dead on real data.

question: Would single-cell resolution lift the per-perturbation floor above pseudobulk?
metric: cross-donor top-50 median + cytokine-gene SNR>3 detectability + go/no-go gate
verdict: NOISE-FLOOR — confirmed floor at pseudobulk depth (x-donor 0.033); only 16% of cytokine genes reach SNR>3; single-cell would not fix it (gate RED).

question: Is per-perturbation recoverability explained by activation-trajectory geometry?
verdict: NEGATIVE — partial ρ≈0 (cond 0.007 p=0.75; gene 0.034 p=0.55); recoverability is NOT a trajectory-geometry artifact.

question: Does donor-CONDITIONING beat donor-AVERAGING for perturbation recovery?
verdict: NO-GO — donor-conditioning (0.016) < donor-averaging (0.034); same-gene concordance is real but floor-magnitude. Conditioning gives no gain.

question: Does any specific-space relational object (similarity/loadings/rank) reach 0.30?
metric: reproducible specific-space contrast vs degree-preserving null
verdict: FAIL — no relational object reaches 0.3 (best = L_loadings_top3 0.111; S-similarity 0.008). Raw-space S≈0.9 is a constant-cosine artifact. Floor is object-general.

question: Do held-out external causal edges reveal a CAUSAL (not just predictive) advantage?
metric: sign-recovery of held-out external edges: causal vs non-causal twin vs deg-preserving null
verdict: IN-DISTRIBUTION — recovers external edge direction above null (0.559>0.500) but with NO causal-specific advantage over the twin (causal−twin -0.010). The C2 edge does not transfer to external causal structure.

question: How much signal is recoverable at all, and where does linear collapse?
reference: gene floor 0.049; ridge 0.02 vs do-op 0.56 of ceiling (gene)
verdict: FLOOR≈0.032 cond / 0.049 gene, real (perm_p 0). A structured residual exists: linear collapses on the gene axis (ridge 0.02 of ceiling) where the do-operator reaches 0.56 — but the pointwise volume is small.

question: Does the SAME null machinery that flags the six negatives ever register a positive?
metric: causal − non-causal twin, Pearson-δ(top-50 DEG), within-distribution hold-outs
verdict: POSITIVE — the do-operator beats its non-causal twin within-distribution (+0.118 condition / +0.162 gene). Signal-detection anchor: the nulls mean 'no signal', not 'no sensitivity'.
